name: Dehumidifiers
summary: Portable and whole-home dehumidifiers, 22-70 pint/day capacity, $200-$1400 range.

evidence.claim: DOE 2019 test procedure (10 CFR Appendix X1) redefined dehumidifier capacity at 65°F/60%RH — the 'DOE new' rating — replacing the older 80°F/60%RH standard. A unit rated 50-pint pre-2019 is typically ~30-pint post-2019.
ENERGY STAR dehumidifiers are 15% more efficient than standard models; annual savings typically $20-$60.
AHAM (Association of Home Appliance Manufacturers) certification program verifies dehumidifier capacity claims against the DOE test procedure.
CPSC 2020 recall of 2 million dehumidifiers (Gree, Midea, others) for fire/burn hazard — among the largest consumer product recalls in US history.

criteria.name: capacity_ppd_doe_new
criteria.notes: Post-2019 DOE rating at 65°F/60%RH (E1). Always compare like-to-like; pre-2019 numbers are inflated by ~50%.

criteria.name: pump_drain
criteria.notes: Pump allows draining uphill; gravity-only drain requires floor-level drain.

criteria.name: operating_temp_low_f
criteria.notes: Basements stay 50-60°F year-round; units rated above 65°F low-op don't work reliably there.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Check whether the 50-pint rating is pre-2019 (80°F/60%RH) or post-2019 DOE-new (65°F/60%RH) (E1). Same unit has both numbers; post-2019 is the fair comparison. A unit marketed as '50-pint' at the same SKU as pre-2019 is typically ~30-pint at modern rating.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the pint capacity the DOE post-2019 figure, or the legacy pre-2019 figure?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Most residential dehumidifiers use refrigerant coils that frost over below ~50°F ambient. Basement use below that threshold requires a desiccant dehumidifier or a unit specifically rated for low temperatures.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What's the minimum operating temperature? Basements below 60°F need a low-temp model.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Dehumidifiers are inherently noisy (50+ dB at high fan). 'Quiet' claims without a specific dB figure are meaningless. 45 dB or below is library-quiet; 55+ dB is noticeable in adjacent rooms.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What's the dB figure at the highest fan setting?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Gree-manufactured dehumidifier model number in the 2013-2020 recall list
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — refer to CPSC recall list (E4) for the exact model and register for replacement

compatibilityQuestions: Square footage of the space you want to dehumidify? Larger spaces need higher capacity.
Basement or above-grade? Basements require low-temp-rated units.
Do you have a floor drain? If not, you need a pump-drain model or you'll be emptying a bucket daily.

repairability.notes: Compressor failures are not economically repairable. Most consumer dehumidifiers are replace-don't-repair after 5-7 years.

representativeSkus.name: 70-Pint TP70WKN (pre-2019-rated)
representativeSkus.notes: Sold as '70-pint' pre-2019 marketing; DOE-new rating is 40 ppd (E1). 5-year compressor warranty.
